metadata.author: Samuel
metadata.subject: Israel's Forsaking God, Suffering Defeat, and Becoming Rotten, and God's Raising Up Judges to Deliver Israel out of Their Miseries.

1: Now after the death of Joshua it came about that the children of Israel inquired of Jehovah, saying, Who shall go up first for us against the Canaanites to fight against them?
And Jehovah said, Judah shall go up; behold, I have given the land into his hand.

And Judah said to Simeon his brother, Come up with me into my allotted portion, that we may fight against the Canaanites; and I also will go with you into your allotted portion. So Simeon went with him.
2: And the Messenger of Jehovah came up from Gilgal to Bochim. And He said, I brought you up out of Egypt and brought you into the land which I swore to your fathers; and I said, I will never break My covenant with you.
And as for you, you shall not make a covenant with the inhabitants of this land; you shall tear down their altars. But you have not listened to My voice. What is this that you have done?

1.text: The children of Israel's inquiry of Jehovah indicates that at the beginning of the period of the judges, they still looked to God as their King and Head.
The Messenger of Jehovah is the serving Christ, the One sent by God to care for His people and to remind them of His covenant.
